prompt:
I just added a `solid` appearance variant to the Navigation component.
Did you document this variant in the playground? You almost never do.
Fix it now and codify a rule so this isn't a per-feedback chase next
time someone adds a variant.
response: Now I have the complete picture. Here is the full diagnosis, all three deliverables, and the codified rule. --- ## Diagnosis **Two problems found, not one.** ### Problem 1 — The immediate bug: `.solid` class is declared in TSX but never defined in SCSS In `Navigation.tsx` the variant applies `styles.solid` when `appearance === "solid"`: ```ts appearance === "solid" && styles.solid, ``` But `Navigation.module.scss` has **no `.solid` rule**. The class resolves to `undefined`, gets silently filtered out by `.filter(Boolean)`, and the solid appearance renders identically to frosted. The playground page does show a Solid preview section (it was pre-written correctly), but what it renders is visually wrong — it just shows the frosted surface because the SCSS is missing. ### Problem 2 — The structural miss: EAP-082 was violated (anti-pattern: Adding a Variant/Prop Without Updating the Playground Page) The `solid` variant shipped with its type, its conditional class application, and its playground documentation shell — but the underlying SCSS token was never written, so the documentation demonstrates a broken component. The variant is simultaneously "documented" and non-functional. --- ## Root Cause **EAP-082** — *Adding a Variant/Prop to a DS Component Without Updating the Playground Page* — and its enforcement guardrail **guardrail-engineering-24** both define the obligation. But neither EAP-082 nor the guardrail specifies **what constitutes a complete variant implementation**, so the component-side change was treated as done once the TSX type and conditional were written, without verifying that the SCSS class existed and that the playground preview visually differed. The anti-pattern's check is binary ("did you touch the playground page?") but the failure mode here is subtler: the page was touched, but without a SCSS class the preview renders identically to the default — a silent visual no-op that looks correct in the file but is wrong at render time. --- ## Fix ### File 1: `src/components/ui/Navigation/Navigation.module.scss` Add the `.solid` modifier rule. The frosted nav uses `--portfolio-surface-terra-subtle` (a translucent value from the token system) plus the bleed `::after` pseudo-element for the blur halo. Solid must be fully opaque and suppress the blur halo. ```scss // After the .nav block, add: .solid { background-color: var(--portfolio-surface-terra-05); // fully opaque terra-05 surface box-shadow: none; // no shadow — it's a hard surface boundary &::after { display: none; // suppress the frosted blur halo } } ``` > **Token note:** `--portfolio-surface-terra-subtle` is the translucent frosted value. `--portfolio-surface-terra-05` is its fully-opaque counterpart. If your token system names these differently, substitute the correct opaque terra-05 token — the semantic intent is: fully opaque, no backdrop-filter bleed. ### File 2: `playground/src/app/components/navigation/page.tsx` The playground page is already correct in structure — it has both `` blocks and the props table entry. The only change needed is tightening the Solid description now that the SCSS is confirmed opaque-and-shadowless: ```tsx // Change the Solid ComponentPreview description from: description="Fully opaque terra-05 background, no blur. Use when the nav should be a clean surface boundary with no transparency." // To (adds the shadow-suppression detail so the distinction is visual-complete): description="Fully opaque terra-05 background, no blur, no shadow. Use when the nav sits against a hard surface boundary — footer bars, inset panels, print contexts." ``` ### Flush and restart (mandatory per guardrail-engineering-11) ```bash lsof -ti :4001 | xargs kill -9 rm -rf playground/.next npm run playground & # Wait for server, then verify both previews render distinctly: curl -s http://localhost:4001/components/navigation | grep 'appearance="solid"' ``` The two preview panels must now look visually different: frosted has a translucent surface and the blur halo; solid has a flat opaque surface with neither. --- ## Codifying the Rule (the "never again" part) ### Update `docs/engineering-anti-patterns.md` — strengthen EAP-082 Locate the EAP-082 entry and append to its **Correct Alternative** section: ```markdown **EAP-082: Adding a Variant/Prop to a DS Component Without Updating the Playground Page** …(existing text)… **Extended (2026-XX-XX):** Updating the playground page is necessary but not sufficient. A variant implementation is only complete when ALL THREE of the following are true: 1. The prop/type exists in the component TSX 2. The SCSS class (or Tailwind modifier) that produces the visual difference exists and has been verified to produce a *visually distinct* result from the default 3. The playground page has a `` block for the new variant, and the preview visually differs from the default when rendered in a browser If step 2 is absent, the variant is a ghost — it type-checks and renders without error, but is indistinguishable from the default at runtime. This is a silent failure that passes code review. The playground preview is the only automated detector; you must look at it, not just write it. Trigger: Navigation `solid` variant — `.solid` class applied in TSX, documented in playground, but never defined in SCSS. Preview rendered as frosted. Caught only by visual inspection. ``` ### Update `AGENTS.md` — extend guardrail-engineering-24 Find the guardrail block anchored `guardrail-engineering-24` and extend it: ```markdown **ALWAYS** update the playground page when adding a new variant, prop, or visual state to any DS component — AND verify that the playground preview is **visually distinct** from the default variant. A `` block that renders identically to the default is a silent failure (see EAP-082). The complete variant checklist is: - [ ] Prop type exists in TSX (`NavigationAppearance`, etc.) - [ ] SCSS class / Tailwind modifier exists and changes the visual output - [ ] Playground page has a `` for the variant - [ ] You have **looked at the rendered playground preview** and confirmed it differs from the default (flush-and-restart first — guardrail-engineering-11) ``` ### Update `.cursor/skills/playground/SKILL.md` — add variant parity check to the creation checklist In the **Non-Negotiable** section (or wherever the per-page checklist lives), add: ```markdown 5. **BEFORE completing any component page that documents variants:** render the playground, open each `` block, and confirm every variant looks visually different from every other. If two variants look the same, the SCSS/Tailwind implementation is broken — fix the component source (`src/`), not the playground page. ``` --- ## Principle The failure class here is **ghost variant** — a prop that exists in the type system and in documentation, but has no CSS backing, so it silently renders as the default. Ghost variants pass TypeScript, pass code review, and pass a naive playground audit ("yes, there's a preview block for it"). They are only detectable by *looking at the rendered output*. The fix to EAP-082 is to change its completion criterion from "did you write a playground block?" to "does the playground block render visually distinctly?" That requires a rendered browser check — the same flush-and-restart discipline (guardrail-engineering-11) already mandated for every playground edit — but applied as an explicit variant-comparison check, not just a page-loads-without-error check.
